{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Benin women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Benin
| Badge              = 
| Nickname           = ''Les Guépards'' <br> (The Cheetahs)<br>Formerly: ''Les Ecureuils'' (The Squirrels)
| Association        = [[Benin Football Federation|Fédération Béninoise de Football]] (FBF, FEBEFOOT)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Sub-confederation  = [[West African Football Union|WAFU]] (West Africa)
| Coach              = [[Gernot Rohr]]
| Captain            = [[Steve Mounié]]
| Most caps          = {{nowrap|[[Stéphane Sessègnon]] (89)}}
| Top scorer         = {{nowrap|[[Stéphane Sessègnon]] (24)}}
| Home Stadium       = [[Stade de l'Amitié]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = BEN
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|BEN}}
| FIFA max           = 59
| FIFA max date      = November–December 2009, April 2010
| FIFA min           = 165
| FIFA min date      = July 1996
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Benin}}
| Elo max            = 56
| Elo max date       = December 1964
| Elo min            = 165
| Elo min date       = 13 May 1995
| pattern_la1        = _zimbabwe1920h
| pattern_b1         = _benin19h
| pattern_ra1        = _zimbabwe1920h
| pattern_sh1        = _benin19h
| pattern_so1        = _benin19h
| leftarm1           = FFF200
| body1              = FFF200
| rightarm1          = FFF200
| shorts1            = FFF200
| socks1             = FFF200
| pattern_la2        = _benin19a
| pattern_b2         = _benin19a
| pattern_ra2        = _benin19a
| pattern_sh2        = _benin19a
| pattern_so2        = _benin19a
| leftarm2           = FEFEFE
| body2              = FEFEFE
| rightarm2          = FEFEFE
| shorts2            = FEFEFE
| socks2             = FEFEFE
| First game         = {{fb|Dahomey}} 0–1 {{fb-rt|NGA|colonial}} <br> ([[Republic of Dahomey|Dahomey]]; 8 November 1959)
| Largest win        = {{fb|Dahomey}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|MTN|1959}} <br> ([[Abidjan]], [[Ivory Coast]]; 27 December 1961)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|NGA|colonial}} 10–1 {{fb-rt|Dahomey}} <br> ([[Colonial Nigeria|Nigeria]]; 28 November 1959)
| World cup apps           = 0
| World cup first          = 
| World cup best           = | Regional name      =[[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 5
| Regional cup first = [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]
| Regional cup best  = Quarter-finals ([[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]])
}}

The '''Benin national football team''' ([[French language|French]]: Équipe nationale de Football du Bénin), nicknamed ''Les Guépards (The Cheetahs)'', represents [[Benin]] in men's international [[association football]] and are controlled by the [[Benin Football Federation]]. They were known as '''Dahomey''' until 1975, when the [[Republic of Dahomey]] became Benin.

Benin have been affiliated with [[FIFA]] since 1962 and are a member of the [[Confederation of African Football]] since 1969. They have never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]], but have participated at four [[Africa Cup of Nations|Africa Cups of Nations]] in [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]], [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]], [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]] and [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]], never placing in the top two in the group stage at all of these occasions. However, Benin has a unique record as the country is the first national team to reach the quarter-finals of an AFCON edition without gaining a single win in their AFCON history.

==History==
Benin hosted its first official international match on 8 November 1959, a 1–0 loss to [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]]. The match was played while the country was still a French dependency, prior to its independence on 1 August 1960.

Benin qualified for the [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations]], their first AFCON in history. However, they lost all three matches to [[South Africa national football team|South Africa]], [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] and again Nigeria. Benin's only goal was scored by [[Moussa Latoundji]] against Nigeria.

History repeated itself again in [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]], when Benin lost to [[Mali national football team|Mali]], the [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]] and yet again Nigeria. They also scored only once through [[Razak Omotoyossi]] in the 4–1 defeat to the Ivory Coast.

In 2010, the [[Benin Football Federation]]'s president [[Anjorin Moucharaf]] was arrested. Members of the BFF decried the imprisonment, saying that Moucharaf had been unjustly accused of fraud, leading to 12 of the 15 board members resigning in protest.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Kobo |first1=Kingsley |title=Fifa back detained Benin FA boss Anjorin Moucharaf |url=https://www.goal.com/en/news/89/africa/2011/08/14/2619508/fifa-back-detained-benin-fa-boss-anjorin-moucharaf |website=Goal |access-date=2021-11-09 |archive-date=2021-11-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109122123/https://www.goal.com/en/news/89/africa/2011/08/14/2619508/fifa-back-detained-benin-fa-boss-anjorin-moucharaf |url-status=live }}</ref>

In the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2010 World Cup qualifiers]], Benin topped their group in the second round. They started with a defeat to [[Angola national football team|Angola]] but went on to win the next four matches and ensure their qualification before the final day. In the third round of the qualifiers, Benin finished second in their group, three points behind [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]]. Despite not qualifying for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]], Benin's second-place finish ensured their qualification to the [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations]], where they drew against [[Mozambique national football team|Mozambique]] to receive their first ever point at the AFCON. The Squirrels then lost their other two matches against Nigeria and defending champions [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]] to finish third in their group and fail to progress to the next round. After this performance, on 8 February 2010, the BFF, not willing to accept a group stage exit for the third time in a row, dissolved the national team and sacked coach [[Michel Dussuyer]], as well as the rest of his staff.<ref>{{Cite news |url=http://www.spiegel.de/sport/fussball/0,1518,676907,00.html |title=Benin löst Nationalelf auf |newspaper=Der Spiegel |date=9 February 2010 |access-date=2010-02-10 |archive-date=2011-06-04 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110604113146/http://www.spiegel.de/sport/fussball/0,1518,676907,00.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.febefoot.net/881-benin-les-ecureuils-et-michel-dussuyer-sanctionnes-.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100211145208/http://www.febefoot.net/881-benin-les-ecureuils-et-michel-dussuyer-sanctionnes-.htm|title=Bénin: Les Ecureuils et Michel DUSSUYER sanctionnés|archive-date=11 February 2010|access-date=21 July 2015|language=fr|publisher=Fédération Béninoise de Football}}</ref> Dussuyer was unaware that he had been sacked and claimed that he had not done anything wrong.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/africa/8502851.stm|title=Benin coach Michel Dussuyer 'unaware' of sacking|date=9 February 2010|access-date=21 July 2015|publisher=BBC Sport|archive-date=23 January 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230123060654/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/africa/8502851.stm|url-status=live}}</ref> The team became an innocent victim of enraged African countries failing to accept defeat at major tournaments and disbanding their national teams in the early 2010s, along with Nigeria, the team that Benin have met in the group stage of all three of their AFCONs before their disbandment, which were suspended for two years by President [[Goodluck Jonathan]] after the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]].

In the second round of the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2014 World Cup qualifiers]], Benin were placed in Group H with [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]], [[Mali national football team|Mali]] and [[Rwanda national football team|Rwanda]]. They finished third in their group, failing to advance to the next round.

On 9 May 2016, FIFA suspended Benin for unknown reasons.

At the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations]], despite advancing only as the third-best third-placed team, Benin reunited with Dussuyer, reached the quarter-finals, where they lost to eventual runners-up [[Senegal national football team|Senegal]], with a shock win over tournament favourites [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] on penalties.

==Kit history==

===Kit manufacturer===
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Kit providers
! Period
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
| 1980–1991
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Puma (brand)|Puma]]
| 1992–1997
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
| 1998–2001
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Erreà]]
| 2002–2005
|-
| {{flagicon|FRA}} [[Airness]]
| 2006–2014
|-
| {{flagicon|NIG}} Tovio
| 2015–2018
|-
| {{flagicon|UK}} [[Umbro]]
| 2019–2022
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Macron (sportswear)|Macron]]
| 2023–present
|}

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Benin national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last twelve months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|date       = 9 June
|time       = {{UTZ|21:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MAR}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1     =
*[[Ayoub El Kaabi|El Kaabi]] {{goal|45+2}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Fez Stadium]]
|location   = [[Fez, Morocco|Fez]], Morocco
|attendance = 34,000
|referee    = Mehrez Melki ([[Tunisian Football Federation|Tunisia]])
|report     = https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/733165/benin-morocco
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C#BEN v ZIM|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 5 September
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|ZIM}}
|goals1     =
*[[Steve Mounié|Mounié]] {{goal|77}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Alassane Ouattara Stadium]]
|location   = [[Abidjan]], Ivory Coast
|attendance = 457
|referee    = Lamin Jammeh ([[Gambian Football Federation|Gambia]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020779
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C#BEN v LES|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 9 September
|time       = {{UTZ|20:45|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 4–0
|team2      = {{fb|LES}}
|goals1     =
*[[Steve Mounié|Mounié]] {{goal|6}}
*[[Andréas Hountondji|Hountondji]] {{goal|23}}
*[[Hassane Imourane|Imourane]] {{goal|33}}
*[[Junior Olaitan|Olaitan]] {{goal|67}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Felix Houphouet Boigny Stadium]]
|location   = [[Abidjan]], Ivory Coast
|attendance = 3,417
|referee    = Mahmood Ali Mahmood Ismail ([[Sudan Football Association|Sudan]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020781
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C#RWA v BEN|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 October
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
|score      = 0–1
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1     = 
|goals2     = 
*[[Tosin Aiyegun|Aiyegun]] {{goal|80}}
|stadium    = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
|location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
|attendance = 
|referee    = Mohamed Maarouf Eid Mansour ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021047
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C#NGA v BEN|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = {{Start date|2025|10|14|df=y}}
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|NGA}}
|score      = 4–0
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1     =
*[[Victor Osimhen|Osimhen]] {{goal|3||37||51}}
*[[Frank Onyeka|Onyeka]] {{goal|90+1}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Godswill Akpabio International Stadium]], [[Uyo]]
|attendance =
|referee    = [[Amin Omar]] ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021045
|result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 18 November
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 3–0
| report     = https://www.panafricafootball.com/post/burkina-faso-4/
| team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Georgi Minoungou|Minoungou]] {{goal|25||45+3}}
*[[Saidou Simpore|Simpore]] {{goal|90+4}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[El Bachir Stadium]]
| location   = [[Mohammedia]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025_Africa_Cup_of_Nations#Group_D|2025 AFCON Group D]]
|date       = 23 December
|time       = {{UTZ|13:30|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|COD}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1     = *[[Théo Bongonda|Bongonda]] {{goal|16}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Al Barid Stadium]] 
|location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance = 13,073
|referee    = [[Abongile Tom]] ([[South African Football Association|South&nbsp;Africa]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506781
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations#Group D|2025 AFCON Group D]]
|date       = 27 December
|time       = {{UTZ|13:30|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|BOT}}
|goals1     = [[Yohan Roche|Roche]] {{goal|28}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Rabat Olympic Stadium]]
|location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance = 7,462
|referee    = [[Shamirah Nabadda]] ([[Federation of Uganda Football Associations|Uganda]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/400132623/1z35p4iuhfxxdfaqjwzkqn2fo/207mir3y73ql1144i13i8xe6s/cfjlin4xytbsucig09ddrlmvo
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025_Africa_Cup_of_Nations#Group_D|2025 AFCON Group D]]
|date       = 30 December
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 0–3
|team2      = {{fb|SEN}}
|goals1     = 
|goals2     = *[[Abdoulaye Seck (footballer, born 1992)|Seck]] {{goal|38}}
*[[Habib Diallo|H. Diallo]] {{goal|62}}
*[[Kalidou Koulibaly|Koulibaly]] {{sentoff|0|71}}
*[[Cherif Ndiaye|C. Ndiaye]] {{goal|90+7}}
|stadium    = [[Ibn Batouta Stadium]]
|location   = [[Tangier]], Morocco
|attendance = 26,707
|referee    = [[Samuel Uwikunda]] ([[Rwanda Football Federation|Rwanda]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506804
|result     = L
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations knockout stage#Round of 16|2025 AFCON Round of 16]]
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|date       = 5 January
|team1      = {{fb-rt|EGY}}
|score      = 3–1
|aet        = yes
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1     = 
*[[Marwan Attia|Attia]] {{goal|69}}
*[[Yasser Ibrahim|Y. Ibrahim]] {{goal|97}}
*[[Mohamed Salah|M. Salah]] {{goal|124}}
|goals2= 
*[[Jodel Dossou|Dossou]] {{goal|83}}
|stadium    = [[Adrar Stadium]]
|location   = [[Agadir]], Morocco
|attendance = 20,191
|referee    = [[Pierre Atcho]] ([[Gabonese Football Federation|Gabon]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506814
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|1}}
|date       = 27 March
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|LBR}}
|goals1     =
*[[Aiyegun Tosin|Tosin]] {{goal|85}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Père Jégo Stadium]]
|location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Jalal Jayed]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|report     = https://www.benin-football.com/actualites/guepards-du-benin-un-test-face-au-liberia-pour-bien-lancer-la-treve-internationale
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|date       = 31 March
|time       = {{UTZ|21:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GUI}}
|score      = 0–1
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Steve Mounié|Mounié]] {{goal|33}}
|stadium    = [[Larbi Zaouli Stadium]]
|location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Haythem Trabelsi]] ([[Tunisian Football Federation|Tunisia]])
|report     = https://sportnewsafrica.com/alertes/amical-la-palestine-et-la-guinee-au-programme-pour-le-benin
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 5 June
|time = {{UTZ|20:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score = 1–1
|team2 = {{fb|NIG}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Amiro Amadou|Amadou]] {{goal|37}}
|goals2 =
*[[Oumar Sako|Sako]] {{goal|90+2}}
|stadium = [[Père Jégo Stadium]]
|location = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = Naoufal Nachite ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]]) 
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-05/benin-vs-niger/4314449
|result = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 9 June
|time = {{UTZ|19:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score = 5–1
|team2 = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Mohamed Tijani|Tijani]] {{goal|39|o.g.}}
*[[Arnaud Komlavi|Komlavi]] {{goal|58}}
*[[Djené]] {{goal|68}}
*[[Kevin Denkey|Denkey]] {{goal|74}}
*[[Kodjo Fo-Doh Laba|Laba]] {{goal|88}}
|goals2 =
*[[Felipe Santos (footballer)|F.Santos]] {{goal|6}}
|stadium = [[El Bachir Stadium]]
|location = [[Mohammedia]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = Mohamed El Baroudi ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]]) 
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-09/togo-vs-benin/4310871
|result = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Burkina Faso
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Benin
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|MTN}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Benin
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MTN}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Mauritania
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|CTA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Benin
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CTA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Central African Republic
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}

==Coaching staff==
[[File:Gernot Rohr 2018.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Gernot Rohr]] is the current manager of Benin]]
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left;"
|-
!'''Position'''
!'''Name'''
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Gernot Rohr]]
|-
|Assistant coaches
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Moustapha Tchawogbé<br>{{flagicon|BEN}} Gervais Adjanohoun
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Apollinaire Gbadamassi
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Ibrahim Kpadenou
|-
|Match Analyst
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Christophe Fagbohoun
|-
|Doctors 
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Dr. Hervé Gbalazou<br>{{flagicon|BEN}} Dr. Michel Djogbenou
|-
|Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Alain Gbenou<br>{{flagicon|BEN}} Thomas Hounkpè<br>{{flagicon|BEN}} Ousmane Houssou<br>{{flagicon|BEN}} Maxime Tchouassi
|-
|Team coordinator 
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Bernard Gbedjissi
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|BEN}} Mohamed Agbassi
|}

===Coaching history===
{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Serge Devèze]]
*{{flagicon|Benin}} [[Wabi Gomez]]
*{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Peter Schnittger]] (1992)
*{{flagicon|Benin}} [[Moise Ekoue]]  (1993)
*{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[Cecil Jones Attuquayefio]] (2003–2004)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Hervé Revelli]] (2004)
*{{flagicon|Benin}} [[Edmé Codjo]] (2005–2007)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Didier Notheaux]] (2007)
*{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Reinhard Fabisch]] (2007–2008)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Michel Dussuyer]] (2008–2010)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Jean-Marc Nobilo]]  (2010)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Denis Goavec]]  (2010–2011)
*{{flagicon|Benin}} [[Edmé Codjo]] (2011–2012)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Manuel Amoros]] (2012–2014)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Didier Ollé-Nicolle]] (2014)
*{{flagicon|Benin}} [[Oumar Tchomogo]] (2015–2017)
*{{flagicon|France}} [[Michel Dussuyer]] (2018–2021)
*{{flagicon|Benin}} [[Moussa Latoundji]] (2022–2023)
*{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Gernot Rohr]] (2023–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] matches againtst [[Palestine national football team|Palestine]] and [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] on 27 and 31 March 2026.<ref>{{cite news |title=La liste des joueurs du Bénin convoqués par Gernot Rohr pour les matchs amicaux face à la Palestine 🇵🇸 le 𝟮𝟳 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝘀 et face à la Guinée 🇬🇳 le 𝟯𝟭 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝘀 ! |url=https://www.instagram.com/p/DV9aOh6jO2o/ |access-date=27 March 2026 |agency=Instagram |publisher=beninfootball |date=16 March 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 5 January 2026, after the match against [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Saturnin Allagbé]]|age={{bda|1993|11|22|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=0|club=Chauray|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Marcel Dandjinou]]|age={{bda|1998|6|25|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=[[Kruger United F.C.|Kruger United]]|clubnat=RSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Serge Obassa|age={{bda|1996|6|30|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Remo Stars F.C.|Remo Stars]]|clubnat=NGA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Olivier Verdon]]|age={{bda|1995|10|5|df=y}}|caps=44|goals=0|club=[[PFC Ludogorets Razgrad|Ludogorets Razgrad]]|clubnat=BUL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Yohan Roche]]|age={{bda|1997|7|7|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=2|club=[[FC Petrolul Ploiești|Petrolul Ploiești]]|clubnat=ROU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mohamed Tijani]]|age={{bda|1997|7|10|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=1|club=[[Yverdon-Sport FC|Yverdon-Sport]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Rachid Moumini]]|age={{bda|2004|10|27|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=1|club=[[Sumgayit FK|Sumgayit]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Rodrigue Fassinou]]|age={{bda|1999|5|22|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[Coton FC|Coton]]|clubnat=BEN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Tamimou Ouorou]]|age={{bda|2003|5|3|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[POFC Botev Vratsa|Botev Vratsa]]|clubnat=BUL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Charlemagne Azongnitode]]|age={{bda|2001|8|8|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Turun Palloseura|TPS]]|clubnat=FIN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Junior Olaitan]]|age={{bda|2002|5|9|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=5|club=[[Beşiktaş J.K.|Beşiktaş]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Dodo Dokou]]|age={{bda|2004|5|4|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=2|club=[[Leixões S.C.|Leixões]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Hassane Imourane]]|age={{bda|2003|4|8|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=2|club=[[Grasshopper Club Zurich|Grasshopper]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mattéo Ahlinvi]]|age={{bda|1999|7|2|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[FC Arsenal Tula|Arsenal Tula]]|clubnat=RUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Rodrigue Kossi]]|age={{bda|1999|12|31|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Hassania Agadir]]|clubnat=MAR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Attidjikou Samadou]]|age={{bda|2004|2|2|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Smouha SC|Smouha]]|clubnat=EGY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Gislain Ahoudo|age={{bda|1999|7|2|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Al-Arabi SC (Saudi Arabia)|Al-Arabi]]|clubnat=KSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Prince Ricardo Dossou|age={{bda|2006|7|20|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=ASVO|clubnat=BEN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jodel Dossou]]|age={{bda|1992|3|17|df=y}}|caps=66|goals=10|club=Pays du Valois|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Steve Mounié]]|age={{bda|1994|9|29|df=y}}|caps=65|goals=22|club=[[Alanyaspor]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Aiyegun Tosin]]|age={{bda|1998|6|26|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=4|club=[[FC Lorient|Lorient]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Rodolfo Aloko]]|age={{bda|2006|12|26|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Charlotte FC|Charlotte]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Michel Boni]]|age={{birth date and age|2008|03|28|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Brentford F.C.|Brentford]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Felipe Santos (footballer)|Felipe Santos]]|age={{bda|1997|1|3|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Araz-Naxçıvan PFK|Araz-Naxçıvan]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for the team within the last 12 months and are still available for selection.
<!--Sorted by position, date of birth, caps, goals and most recent call-up.-->
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=Mariano Tchinonvi|age={{bda|2004|7|29|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Loto-Popo FC|Loto-Popo]]|clubnat=BEN|latest=v. {{fb|BFA}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=12|pos=DF|name=[[David Kiki]]|age={{bda|1993|11|25|df=y}}|caps=54|goals=0|club=[[FCSB]]|clubnat=ROU|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Rabiou Sankamao|age={{bda|2003|10|12|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Wydad de Fès]]|clubnat=MAR|latest=v. {{fb|BFA}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Jordan Lawson|age={{bda|2007|8|7|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Lincoln Trail College]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|BFA}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Cédric Hountondji]]|age={{bda|1994|1|19|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=2|club=[[Bandırmaspor]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|NGA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Gabriel Moulero|age={{bda|2006|1|1|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Dila Gori|Dila Gori II]]|clubnat=GEO|latest=v. {{fb|MAR}}, 9 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=15|pos=MF|name=[[Sessi D'Almeida]]|age={{bda|1995|11|20|df=y}}|caps=45|goals=1|club=[[Neftçi PFK|Neftçi]]|clubnat=AZE|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=24|pos=MF|name=[[Razack Rachidou]]|age={{bda|2006|6|22|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[NK Kustošija|Kustošija]]|clubnat=CRO|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=22|pos=MF|name=Romaric Amoussou|age={{bda|2000|12|10|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[ASEC Mimosas]]|clubnat=CIV|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=25|pos=MF|name=[[Olatoundji Tessilimi]]|age={{bda|1998|2|18|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Seinäjoen Jalkapallokerho|SJK]]|clubnat=FIN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Mariano Ahouangbo]]|age={{bda|2002|11|16|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[NK Olimpija Ljubljana|Olimpija Ljubljana]]|clubnat=SVN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Salifu Ibrahim]]|age={{bda|2000|6|6|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Free agent|''Unattached'']]|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|BFA}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=28|pos=FW|name=[[Adam Akimey]]|age={{bda|2004|2|25|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Helsingborgs IF|Helsingborg]]|clubnat=SWE|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Andréas Hountondji]]|age={{bda|2002|7|11|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=3|club=[[FC St. Pauli]]|clubnat=GER|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Candas Fiogbé]]|age={{bda|2005|1|18|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[PAS Giannina F.C.|PAS Giannina]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|5 January 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/benin-recintlp.html |title=Benin&nbsp;– Record International Players |author=Mamrud, Roberto |access-date=12 November 2017 |archive-date=7 July 2018 |website=[[RSSSF]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180707010350/http://www.rsssf.com/miscellaneous/benin-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Benin.''

[[File:Stéphane Sessègnon.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Stéphane Sessègnon is Benin's top goalscorer and their most capped player.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
|+Most appearances
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Stéphane Sessègnon]] || 89 || 24 || 2004–2023
|-
| 2 ||align=left| [[Khaled Adénon]] || 86 || 2 || 2006–2023
|-
| 3 ||align=left| '''[[Jodel Dossou]]''' || 76 || 11 || 2013–present
|-
| rowspan=2|4 ||align=left| '''[[Steve Mounié]]''' || 69 || 22 || 2015–present 
|-
|align=left| [[Mickaël Poté]] || 69 || 10 || 2008–2022
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Damien Chrysostome]] || 58 || 0 || 2002–2011
|-
| 7 ||align=left| '''[[David Kiki]]''' || 56 || 0 || 2015–present 
|-
| rowspan=2|8 ||align=left| [[Jocelyn Ahouéya]] || 55 || 3 || 2003–2013
|-
|align=left| [[Razak Omotoyossi]] || 55 || 21 || 2004–2016 
|-
| 10 ||align=left| [[Romuald Boco]] || 52 || 1 || 2004–2013
|-
|}

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
|+Top goalscorers
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Stéphane Sessègnon]] || 24 || 89 || {{#expr:24/89 round 2}} || 2004–2023
|-
| 2 ||align=left| '''[[Steve Mounié]]''' || 22 || 69 || {{#expr:22/69 round 2}} || 2015–present
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[Razak Omotoyossi]] || 21 || 55 || {{#expr:21/55 round 2}} || 2004–2016
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Oumar Tchomogo]] || 15 || 34 || {{#expr:15/34 round 2}} || 1995–2008
|-
| 5 ||align=left| '''[[Jodel Dossou]]''' || 11 || 76 || {{#expr:11/76 round 2}} || 2013–present
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Mickaël Poté]] || 10 || 69 || {{#expr:10/69 round 2}} || 2008–2022
|-
| rowspan=3|7 ||align=left| [[Moussa Latoundji]] || 6 || 21 || {{#expr:6/21 round 2}} || 1993–2004
|-
|align=left| [[Mouritala Ogunbiyi]] || 6 || 47 || {{#expr:6/47 round 2}} || 1998–2019
|-
|align=left| [[Séïdath Tchomogo]] || 6 || 51 || {{#expr:6/51 round 2}} || 2003–2014
|-
| rowspan=3|10 ||align=left| [[Léon Bessan]] || 5 || 20 || {{#expr:5/20 round 2}} || 1995–2007
|-
| align=left| '''[[Junior Olaitan]]''' || 5 || 40 || {{#expr:5/40 round 2}} || 2021–present
|-
|align=left| [[Anicet Adjamossi]] || 5 || 48 || {{#expr:5/48 round 2}} || 2002–2013
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=40|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to {{flagicon|Sweden}} [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member; part of France''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member; part of France''
|-
!colspan=9|as '''{{fb|Dahomey}}'''
!colspan=6|as '''{{fb|Dahomey}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|England}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]] and {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 10
|-
!colspan=9|as '''{{fbicon|BEN|1975}} / {{fb|BEN}}'''
!colspan=6|as '''{{fbicon|BEN|1975}} / {{fb|BEN}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Argentina|1861}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]] and {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 0
| 6
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
| 6
| 1
| 0
| 5
| 3
| 19
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 1
| 2
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 12
| 2
| 3
| 7
| 13
| 26
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 12
| 7
| 1
| 4
| 18
| 14
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 6
| 2
| 2
| 2
| 8
| 9
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 2
| 1
| 0
| 1
| 2
| 3
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|6
|3
|1
|2
|5
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10
|5
|2
|3
|12
|11
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!−
!{{Tooltip|0/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!−
!−
!−
!−
!−
!−
!60
!21
!10
!29
!63
!104
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{main|Benin at the Africa Cup of Nations}}

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|UAR}} [[1959 Africa Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|SDN|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|NGA}} [[1980 Africa Cup of Nations|1980]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|LBY|1977}} [[1982 Africa Cup of Nations|1982]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[1984 Africa Cup of Nations|1984]]||colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[1988 Africa Cup of Nations|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ALG}} [[1990 Africa Cup of Nations|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|SEN}} [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN|1959}} [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations|1994]]
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|BFA}} [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations|1998]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} {{flagicon|NGA}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MLI}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]||Group stage||16th||3||0||0||3||1||8
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]||rowspan=2|Group stage||15th||3||0||0||3||1||7
|-
|{{flagicon|ANG}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]||14th||3||0||1||2||2||5
|-
|{{flagicon|EQG}} {{flagicon|GAB}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|EQG}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|GAB}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]||'''Quarter-finals'''||8th||5||0||4||1||3||4
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]||rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]||Round of 16
|14th
|4
|1
|0
|3
|2
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|KEN}} {{flagicon|TAN}} {{flagicon|UGA}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total
!Quarter-finals
!5/35
!18
!1
!5
!12
!9
!31
|}

{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===West African Nations Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[West African Nations Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|BEN|1975}} [[1982 West African Nations Cup|1982]]
|Group stage
|6th
|3
|0
|2
|1
|4
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[1983 West African Nations Cup|1983]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|BFA}} [[1984 West African Nations Cup|1984]]
|Group stage
|5th
|4
|0
|0
|4
|3
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[1986 West African Nations Cup|1986]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|LBR}} [[1987 West African Nations Cup|1987]]
|Group stage
|7th
|3
|0
|2
|1
|3
|7
|-
!Total
!Group stage
!3/5
!10
!0
!4
!6
!10
!24
|-
|}
{{col-2}}

===WAFU Nations Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[WAFU Nations Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|NGA}} [[2010 WAFU Nations Cup|2010]]
|Group stage
|7th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|0
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|NGA}} [[2011 WAFU Nations Cup|2011]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[2013 WAFU Nations Cup|2013]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''4'''
|'''0'''
|'''2'''
|'''2'''
|'''5'''
|'''6'''
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[2017 WAFU Cup of Nations|2017]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''5'''
|'''2'''
|'''0'''
|'''3'''
|'''4'''
|'''8'''
|-
!Total
!Fourth place
!3/4
!12
!2
!3
!7
!9
!19
|}
{{col-end}}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[UEMOA Tournament]]'''
**  {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): 2013

== References ==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category|Benin national association football team}}
* [http://bjfoot.com/ Football au Bénin] {{in lang|fr}}
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/ben/ Benin] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/BEN Benin] at [[FIFA]]
* [http://www.beninfootball.com Le miroir du football béninois!] {{in lang|fr}}

{{Benin national football team}}
{{Navboxes
|title=Benin squads
|bg = #FCD116
|fg = #008751
|bordercolor = #E8112D
|list1=
{{Benin squad 2004 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Benin squad 2008 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Benin squad 2010 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Benin squad 2019 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Benin squad 2025 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Benin national football team managers}}
}}
{{Navboxes|title=Finalists|list1=
{{2010 Africa Cup of Nations finalists}}
}}
{{Football in Benin}}
{{WAFU Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Benin}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Benin National Football Team}}
[[Category:Benin national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
